channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Supreme Court Allows Abortion Pill Access by Mail to Continue
item.description: A federal appeals court ruling against the Food and Drug Administration would have restricted access by mail to mifepristone.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:description: The clash over access to mifepristone has put the Trump administration in a difficult political position ahead of the midterm election.

item.title: Mail-Order Abortions Have Quickly Become Common
item.description: Pills shipped to states with bans are a reason abortion has increased since Roe v. Wade was overturned.
item.dc:creator: Margot Sanger-Katz and Claire Cain Miller

item.title: What to Know About the Latest Abortion Pill Lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that mifepristone could continue to be prescribed by telemedicine while a lower court reviews the case.
item.dc:creator: Pam Belluck

item.title: Justices Allow Abortion Pill Access by Mail to Continue
item.description: Also, this is a big week for the art market. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen
item.media:credit: Hannah Yoon for The New York Times

item.title: Iran War Powers Vote Fails Again in Senate, but GOP Opposition Grows
item.description: Senator Lisa Murkowski of Alaska became the latest Republican to switch her vote to halt the conflict and require President Trump to win congressional approval to continue it.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Lisa Murkowski, Republican of Alaska, during a committee hearing. She previously opposed similar resolutions.

item.title: Trump Was Flattering, Xi Was Resolute. The Difference Spoke Volumes.
item.description: In contrast to his rhetoric about China at home, President Trump spoke in conciliatory terms with Xi Jinping, the Chinese leader.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.media:description: President Trump with President Xi Jinping of China in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Trump’s and Xi’s Body Language at the Summit Mirrored Their Styles
item.description: Although at odds over issues like trade and Taiwan, the U.S. and Chinese leaders met in Beijing with a show of friendly gestures.
item.dc:creator: Alan Yuhas

item.title: Spies, Sanctions, Cyberattacks: China and the U.S. Clash Behind the Scenes
item.description: After months of avoiding confrontation, the Trump administration has taken recent steps to call out China on Iran, artificial intelligence and spying.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Dustin Volz
item.media:description: President Trump met with President Xi Jinping of China on Thursday after a flurry of actions from his administration in recent weeks against China.

item.title: C.I.A. Director Visits Cuba as Tensions Rise and Island Runs Out of Oil
item.description: John Ratcliffe, the C.I.A. director, is the highest-ranking official in the Trump administration to visit the country.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Michael Crowley and Frances Robles
item.media:credit: Lisette Poole for The New York Times
item.media:description: A gas station in Havana this month. In January, the Trump administration cut off oil shipments from Venezuela, which had been supplying a large amount of Cuba’s oil.

item.title: Life, Death and Rebirth in Nepal, the Land of the Buddha
item.description: Starting at the birthplace of Buddhism, a writer traces how its teachings spread across Asia, transforming the continent forever.
item.dc:creator: Aatish Taseer and Keerthana Kunnath

item.title: Mystery Grows Around Representative Thomas Kean Jr.’s Absence
item.description: Representative Thomas Kean Jr. last voted in Washington on March 5, citing a medical issue. An appearance planned for late May has been canceled.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully
item.media:credit: Bill Clark/CQ Roll Call, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Thomas Kean Jr., a Republican congressman from New Jersey, has been out of the public eye for more than two months.

item.title: James Talarico’s Taco Order Sparks Debate in Texas Senate Race
item.description: What started as a taco stop with former President Barack Obama quickly turned into a very Texas debate over the proper breakfast taco order.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Joel Angel Juarez-Pool
item.media:description: Former President Barack Obama ordering tacos alongside Texas Senate candidate James Talarico at the restaurant Taco Joint in Austin earlier this month.

item.title: South Carolina Governor Formally Calls for Redistricting Special Session
item.description: But he stopped short of ordering lawmakers to redo the map so that Republicans could sweep the state’s congressional delegation, which President Trump has pressured them to do.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina
item.media:description: Gov. Henry McMaster of South Carolina at a news conference last year in Columbia, S.C.

item.title: Green Card Holders Targeted for Deportation by New ‘Removal Apparatus’
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security recently formed a unit tasked with revetting thousands of immigrants with permanent residency.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo
item.media:description: About 2,890 cases had been reviewed or were still being assessed by the new unit under U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services as of May 7.

item.title: Vance Blames Democrats as U.S. Halts Some Medicaid Payments, Claiming Fraud
item.description: Vice President JD Vance traveled to Maine for a speech, making a midterm election pitch that only Republicans could root out fraud in public benefits.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance speaking in Bangor, Maine, on Thursday.

item.title: Why Trump’s Reflecting Pool Repairs Are in Trouble
item.description: Federal records show that the no-bid contract to repair the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool and paint it blue now costs $13.1 million. That’s about seven times the amount President Trump initially said it would cost.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold, Nikolay Nikolov, Gilad Thaler, Rafaela Balster, Léo Hamelin, Stephanie Swart and Whitney Shefte

item.title: U.S. Border Patrol Chief Resigns in Latest Homeland Security Shake-Up
item.description: Michael Banks is the latest high-profile official to leave the Department of Homeland Security amid President Trump’s immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.media:description: Michael Banks, who resigned as U.S. Border Patrol chief on Thursday, near the border in Sunland Park, N.M., last year.

item.title: Justice Dept. Accuses Yale Medical School of Discriminating Against White and Asian Applicants
item.description: The finding was the second time in eight days that the Trump administration had targeted a major medical school over admissions policies.
item.dc:creator: Michael C. Bender
item.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
item.media:description: Harmeet K. Dhillon, the assistant attorney general for civil rights, said in a letter that since a Supreme Court ruling barring race-conscious admissions policies, Yale employed a holistic review process “to uncover and then use applicants’ race through direct and indirect means.”

item.title: Inside the Secret Mission to Fly Taiwan’s President to Africa
item.description: From satellite phone check-ins to a borrowed royal plane, new details show how Taiwan’s leader’s team outwitted China and pulled off an audacious journey to southern Africa.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley and Amy Chang Chien
item.media:credit: Taiwan Presidential Office, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A photograph provided by the Taiwan Presidential Office showed the Taiwanese leader, Lai Ching-te, waving upon arriving in Eswatini in May.

item.title: UCF Commencement Speaker Draws Boos After A.I. Remarks
item.description: Humanities students made their displeasure known at the University of Central Florida.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Gershenson

item.title: OpenAI Trial Heads to Jury After Closing Arguments in Musk vs. Altman Case
item.description: Lawyers for Elon Musk and OpenAI made their closing arguments in the blockbuster federal trial on Thursday. Nine jurors are set to begin deliberations next week.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lawyers for Elon Musk and OpenAI made their closing arguments in an Oakland federal court on Thursday.

item.title: OpenAI Considers Legal Action Against Apple in Strained Relationship
item.description: The A.I. company, which is in the middle of a court fight with Elon Musk, has been unhappy with how Apple has integrated ChatGPT into its devices.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang
item.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Apple Store in Grand Central Terminal in Manhattan. Apple struck a deal two years ago to support its devices with OpenAI’s ChatGPT chatbot.

item.title: Is Musk Running an Illegal Power Plant? The D.O.J. Says It Might Weigh In.
item.description: In a court filing, the administration signaled support for Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company in a lawsuit challenging xAI’s generators at a huge Mississippi data center.
item.dc:creator: Hiroko Tabuchi
item.category: National Assn for the Advancement of Colored People
item.media:credit: Brad J. Vest for The New York Times
item.media:description: The entrance to xAI’s power plant in Southaven, Miss., in February.

item.title: Hantavirus Doesn’t Spread Easily, but Officials May Be Downplaying Risks
item.description: The virus is clearly far less contagious than the coronavirus, scientists agree, but they have found cases where it spread among people without direct contact.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: Passengers from the MV Hondius evacuated from the cruise ship this week in Tenerife, Spain.

item.title: 16 More People in the U.S. Are Being Monitored for Hantavirus, C.D.C. Says
item.description: They were passengers on a plane to Johannesburg with an infected Dutch woman who later died.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: A person in a biohazard suit at the airport in Omaha, Neb., as passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius arrived on Monday.

item.title: Will Her Daughter Be Safe at Pali High as It Rebuilds From LA Wildfires?
item.description: It’s a nightmare faced by families all around Los Angeles: After wildfire smoke blanketed homes, schools and offices with toxic chemicals, when is it OK to go back?
item.dc:creator: Emily Baumgaertner Nunn and Jenna Schoenefeld
item.category: Palisades Charter High School (Pacific Palisades, Calif)
item.media:credit: Jenna Schoenefeld for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pearl Abrahams, a senior at Palisades Charter High School in California, attended classes — along with 2,400 other students — in an abandoned department store while her school, burned in the Palisades Fire last year, was rehabilitated.

item.title: Australian Jews Testify on Experiences of Antisemitism in Bondi Beach Inquiry
item.description: As part of a public inquiry into the massacre at Bondi Beach, dozens have described being targeted for their Jewish identity in their daily lives.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.media:credit: Matthew Abbott for The New York Times
item.media:description: A menorah at a park overlooking Sydney Harbour last December, a few days after the attack at Bondi Beach.

item.title: Inside Jack Schlossberg’s Chaotic Campaign to Revive Camelot
item.description: Erratic behavior and staff turnover have colored Mr. Schlossberg’s bid for a House seat in New York, raising questions about his readiness for office.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos
item.media:description: Jack Schlossberg, a Kennedy heir who is running to represent much of Manhattan in Congress, has churned through campaign staff at an unusual pace.

item.title: Bumble Is Killing the Swipe Feature. What’s Next for Dating Apps?
item.description: Bumble is losing the feature that reshaped how we look for love. Daters may be ready for something more intentional.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Pearson

item.title: Thomas Massie Is One of a Vanishing Breed: A Republican Who Will Stand Up to Trump
item.description: An individual with principles and quirks and an aversion to being told what to do.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Mangu-Ward
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: Another Trump Appointee Learns It’s Easier to Be a Critic Than a Leader
item.description: We have no F.D.A. director, no permanent C.D.C. director and no surgeon general.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Jetelina
item.media:credit: Sarah van Rij for The New York Times

item.title: What Are Your Evening Rituals? We Want to Know.
item.description: Do you meditate? Play Wordle? Wear soft pajamas and sip chamomile tea? We’d like to hear about the little routines that help you wind down.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Schupak

item.title: Flag With Swastikas and Star of David Flown at N.Y.U., Police Say
item.description: The flag appeared on a university building in the heart of Greenwich Village during a graduation week event, and resembled the purple N.Y.U. banners flying on campus.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy
item.media:description: A flag with swastikas and a Star of David was taken down from a building on the New York University campus.

item.title: Plane Crashes Into House in Ohio, Killing 2
item.description: No one on the ground was hurt when a small plane crashed into a house on a residential street south of downtown Akron, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk and Alexandra E. Petri
item.media:description: Two people were killed when a single-engine Piper PA-28 Cherokee crashed into a house in Akron, Ohio, on Thursday afternoon, officials said. No one on the ground was injured.

item.title: G. Robert Blakey Dies at 90; Drafted the RICO Anti-Racketeering Statute
item.description: He was also the chief counsel to the House Select Committee on Assassinations in the late 1970s and spent years as a Notre Dame law professor.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir
item.media:description: G. Robert Blakey in 1985. With RICO, he helped create “a new form of jurisprudence,” said Ronald Goldstock, a former director of the Organized Crime Task Force in New York. “It was such a dramatic change that prosecutors didn’t initially understand or use it.”

item.title: Chud the Builder, Known for Racist Confrontations, Charged With Attempted Murder
item.description: Dalton Eatherly, who goes by Chud the Builder, was arrested after an altercation that ended in gunfire outside a courthouse near Nashville.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin
item.media:description: A Sheriff’s deputy investigates a shooting scene outside the Montgomery County Courthouse in Clarksville, Tenn.
